mainEntity.name: Will adding per-page JSON-LD schema actually improve my SEO rankings or is it just a cosmetic rich snippet thing?

acceptedAnswer.text:
A solid per-page JSON-LD setup usually boosts rich result eligibility and click‑through rates rather than raw rankings by itself. You’ll typically see impact in how your listings look (stars, FAQs, breadcrumbs) before you see traffic changes.

Structured data is a clarity signal: it helps Google understand entities, relationships, and page purpose. When that understanding is high and content quality is good, Google is more willing to show enhanced results, which attract more clicks.

KEY FACTORS
- Correct schema type & required properties filled.
- Clean, error‑free markup validated in testing tools.
- Schema content aligned with real on‑page content.
- Overall site quality, speed, and link profile.

Implement JSON-LD page by page, then watch Search Console’s enhancements and performance reports for impressions, CTR, and rich result coverage. Treat schema as an SEO multiplier, not a standalone ranking hack.

Using a generator that enforces required fields can reduce errors and speed up seeing those rich result benefits.
acceptedAnswer.description: Per-page JSON-LD mainly improves *eligibility for rich results* and click‑through rates, not rankings in isolation. When combined with strong content and technical SEO, better structured data can indirectly support rankings by improving relevance signals and engagement.
